agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 regions

report.hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 regions by introducing bulky hydrophobic residues at positions 5 and 11 will increase selectivity margin without compromising SSTR2 binding affinity.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current designs are achieving strong binding to SSTR2 but are not sufficient

report.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong binding to SSTR2 but are not sufficiently differentiating from off-targets. The focus should shift to fine-tuning non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are known to mediate off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved hydrophobic and electrostatic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets, which are likely responsible for off-target binding.
